workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly - like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats - things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat - where I do the same sequence of actions (e.g., get email -> schedule meeting -> create doc)?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Eval Work-Item Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: Dozens of distinct messages across tasks, requirements, bugs, and epics were sent within the last few days. Many are part of the same evaluation infrastructure / eval coverage / IcM evals theme, creating repeated cognitive load even when skimmed. Approx. 30-45 minutes total.

signals.title: Eval Strategy & Leadership Thread Engagement
signals.rawExcerpt: Long-form, high-signal threads (updates, feedback, strategy, kudos). Multiple replies and forwards indicate active participation. Subject patterns include: Re: Customer donated evals added Playbook, Fw: Announcing EvalCon, Re: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive. Approx. 45-60 minutes.

signals.title: SEVAL Incident Announcement Monitoring
signals.rawExcerpt: Clustered updates (initial alert to mitigation to closure). Subject pattern: SEVAL Incident Announcement, Update: issue has been mitigated. Mostly scanning for status + resolved / action required. Approx. 10-15 minutes.

signals.title: Internal Events & Training Comms Digest Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: ESS - MCS Evals - Hands-on Training, BIC All Hands Pre-event reminders, Use Copilot Tasks Today, Microsoft Daily / Weekly Digest, CAPE Events Updates. Mostly skim / decide attend vs ignore. Approx. 10-20 minutes.

signals.title: Compliance & Security Alert Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: Action Required: File Blocked - sensitive data, GitHub admin elevation/revocation notices, Loop workspace compliance warnings. Mostly quick triage. Approx. 5-10 minutes.

signals.title: Evals Governance Multi-Party Email Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple Re: iterations; several CCs added as discussion progressed. Responses include clarifications about engagement tracking, advisory vs non-advisory work, and process alignment. Core topic: How customer-donated evaluations should be represented in the Playbook, including governance, ownership, and how engagements are tracked internally.

signals.title: EvalCon Content Proposal Coordination Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Forwarded message followed by discussion and side pings. Tight deadlines prompted multiple replies and coordination. Continued discussion spilled into Teams. Core topic: Whether and how CAPE should participate in EvalCon, what customer-facing eval stories to present, aligning on ownership and submission timing.

signals.title: US Pod Stand Up (Weekly Tuesday)
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand Up - Tuesdays, 12:35-1:00 PM. Consistent core group. Recurs weekly with the same small-team cohort.

signals.title: Learning & Scaling Fridays Deep-Work Session
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays - PST, Fridays 2:05-5:00 PM. Small, fixed group (immediate team). Recurs weekly.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting - Mondays, 8:05-8:30 AM. Stable project v-team with recurring contributors; clear weekly cadence.

signals.title: CAPE / Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync (Weekly Friday)
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE / Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync - Fridays, 10:00-11:00 AM. Repeats weekly with a consistent cross-functional customer-engagement group.

signals.title: MCS Analytics & Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review (Weekly Wednesday)
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review - Wednesdays, 9:00-9:30 AM. Same PM + analytics leadership cohort every week.

signals.title: CAPE Architecture Call (Weekly Monday)
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call - Mondays, 9:05-10:00 AM. Large but stable CAPE-wide attendee set; very consistent weekly time.

signals.title: SEVAL / BizChat Office Hours (Weekly Multi-Region)
participants: Hundreds of field and engineering attendees
signals.rawExcerpt: SEVAL / BizChat Office Hours (US & EU/Asia) - weekly, hundreds of attendees. Open office hours format.

signals.title: External Customer-Facing Meetings (Recurring Weekly Pattern)
signals.rawExcerpt: Meetings with at least one external domain: 8. Total external-facing time: approx 7 hours 45 minutes. Examples of external domains: Accenture, Avanade, Cognizant, IBM, KPMG, EY, Hitachi Solutions. Each typically blocked ~55-60 minutes.

signals.title: Recurring Evals Strategy Q&A (PM as Source of Truth)
signals.rawExcerpt: People frequently ask to interpret, frame, or sanity-check evaluation strategy. Typical asks: how to set realistic eval targets; how evals fit into agent development lifecycle; how to explain eval-driven development to customers. Direct prompts like how have you been talking about this with customers. Pattern: treated as the source of truth for eval philosophy.

signals.title: Eval Product Judgment & Triage (Bug vs Limitation vs Design)
signals.rawExcerpt: People come when documentation or UI is not enough. Recurring: which eval type to use for a given scenario; whether specific features are preview vs GA; whether a behavior is a bug, limitation, or expected design; how to interpret inconsistent eval results across tenants.

signals.title: Customer Eval Escalation & Unblocking (De Facto DRI)
signals.rawExcerpt: You are repeatedly pulled in when a customer eval is blocked or failing; an ICM/ADO item needs triage; the field needs help reproducing or escalating. Visible in customer-named threads and DRI/TPM channels. Pattern: acting as de facto DRI for eval health and customer unblockers.

signals.title: Field Enablement Deck & FAQ Authoring Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: After sessions, people repeatedly come back for slides or decks that summarize evals clearly; help answering field FAQs; guidance on depth for different audiences; validation that messaging to customers is correct. Pattern: seen as both teacher and editor for eval narratives.

signals.title: Technical Artifact Sharing (Canonical Link Hub)
signals.rawExcerpt: You often share or reference concrete working materials, especially when unblocking others. Commonly shared: GitHub repos and internal tools related to skills, evals, or playbooks; SharePoint files, ZIPs, decks, and Loop components. Pattern: frequently act as hub for here is the latest version or here is the canonical link.

signals.title: Self-Posted Status & Reflection Updates (Agent Channel)
signals.rawExcerpt: You also post structured updates and notes in Serena-Agent / Status Update threads; self-directed notes about side projects, skills, and reflections on work patterns. These are less conversational but recurring and substantive.

signals.title: Skill-Detector Technical Artifact Iteration
signals.rawExcerpt: Archives and packages: ZIP files related to the skill-detector project (skill-detector.zip, skill-detector 1.zip). Scripts and configs: Shell scripts (.sh), JSON config/state files, database files (.db). Your recent work heavily involved building, running, or debugging tooling, rather than purely reviewing prose documents.

signals.title: SKILL.md Spec / Capability Definition Authoring
signals.rawExcerpt: Markdown-based documentation embedded in repositories (e.g., SKILL.md inside the skill-detector artifacts). These function as specs or capability definitions, even though they are not Word docs or formal PRDs.

signals.title: HTML Dashboard Authoring & Refresh (Skilluminator & CAPE Eval)
signals.rawExcerpt: HTML dashboard: Skilluminator Cycle 3 Dashboard and CAPE_Eval_Dashboard. You own and refresh these when eval enablement content or metrics change. Update pattern: iterative / ongoing (multiple versions shared). Regular cycle updates.

signals.title: CAPE Eval Engagement Tracker (Loop) Weekly Update
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Eval Engagement Tracker. Type: Loop document (tracker). Purpose: Tracks active customer eval engagements, agent type, tooling, and engagement notes. Update pattern: Ongoing / weekly as new customers or status changes occur. You are a co-author.

signals.title: Weekly Status Report Contribution (CAPE/BAT Loop)
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Status Reports (CAPE Compete, BAT, etc.). Type: Loop-based weekly status reports. Purpose: Cross-team status, milestones, highlights/lowlights. Update pattern: Weekly. Your role: you frequently contribute content, review, or share these reports as part of CAPE rhythms.

signals.title: Eval Template & Guidance Artifact Reuse / Curation
signals.rawExcerpt: Templates and Guidance Artifacts You Reuse: Eval templates, Enablement decks, Internal guidance documents. Recur whenever a new customer engagement starts, a new eval capability launches, or you run enablement or field-facing sessions. Agent Eval Sets_Template.xlsx seen in recent file activity.

signals.title: Repeating Workflow: Notification to Review Meeting to Loop Doc
signals.rawExcerpt: Typical sequence: Notification / email to Review meeting to Loop doc updated or created. You receive emails or system notifications related to reviews. Those topics appear in recurring or ad-hoc review meetings. In those meetings, Loop pages or Word docs are created or shared, authored or edited by you.

signals.title: Parallel Eval Artifact Recreation Across Team Members
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple people independently create or refine: Eval checklists (Word), Eval set templates (Excel), Eval scorecards (PowerPoint), Decision trees for metric selection (Figma). Often merging earlier versions, streamlining, or combining two worlds after the fact. The intent is clearly shared; differences are mostly format and sequencing, not philosophy.

signals.title: Cross-Surface Status Update Reformatting (Loop to Slides to Email)
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated requests to summarize progress, re-group feedback into themes, populate Loop pages, slides, or newsletters with what is done / next / blockers. Often the same content is rewritten for different surfaces (Loop to slides to email). The structure of the update is stable; the friction is in reformatting, not thinking.

signals.title: Customer Feedback Synthesis (Repeated From-Scratch Pattern)
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated asks to: read long docs or comments; summarize into themes; list impacted customers; tie feedback to actions. You are explicitly asked to do this in a specific format during high-priority moments. The desired output format is known; the work is cognitively expensive but structurally predictable.
